title: Thanjavooru Mannu Eduthu Lyrics Porkkaalam
stanzas: <div>Thanjavooru Mannu Eduthu Lyrics Porkkaalam

Thanjavuru mannu eduthu
thamarabarani thanniya vittu

Thanjavuru mannu eduthu
thamarabarani thanniya vittu
serthu serthu senjathindha bomma
idhu bomma illa bomma illa unmai

eththanaiyo bomma
senjen kannamaa(2)
adi aththanaiyum
unnapola minnumaa
padhil sollammaa.

thanthaaney thanthaaney
thanthaana kuyile
saami thanthaaney thanthaaney
ennoda mayile.(2)

mookku senja
mannu adhu moonaaru.
pattu kannam senja
mannu adhu ponnooru.

kadhu senja mannu
adhu maelooru.
ava udhadu senja
mannu mattum thaenooru.

karuppu koondhal senjadhu
karisa patti mannunga.
thanga kazhuthu senjathu
sangagiri mannunga.

vaayazhagu senjathellaam
vaigai aaththu mannunga.
pallazhagu senjathu
mullaiyooru mannunga.

neththi seiyum mannukku
suththi suththi vandhenga
nilavil manneduthu
neththi senjen paarunga.

thanthaaney thanthaaney
thanthaana kuyile
saami thanthaaney thanthaaney
ennoda mayile.(2)

Thanjavuru mannu eduthu
thamarabarani thanniya vittu
serthu serthu senjathindha bomma
idhu bomma illa bomma illa unmai

thangavayal manneduthen
tholukku.
naan thamarapadi manneduthen
thanathukku.

vazhaiyoothi manneduthen
vayiththukku.
ada kanjanooru manneduthen
iduppukku.

kanjipuram veedhiyila
manneduthen kaigalukku.
srirangam manneduthen
chinna ponnu viralukku.

pattu kottai oadaiyila
manneduthen kaalukku.
paanjaalam kurichiyila
manneduthen nagathukku.

oorellaam manneduthu
uruvam thandhen udambukku.
en usura naan koduthu
usuru thandhen kannukku.

thanthaaney thanthaaney
thanthaana kuyile
saami thanthaaney thanthaaney
ennoda mayile.

podey..
Thanjavuru mannu eduthu
thamarabarani thanniya vittu
serthu serthu senjathindha bomma
idhu bomma illa bomma illa unmai

eththanaiyo bomma
senjen kannamaa(2)
adi aththanaiyum
unnapola minnumaa
padhil sollammaa.

thanthaaney thanthaaney
thanthaana kuyile
saami thanthaaney thanthaaney
ennoda mayile.(2)

enrichedMetadata.faq:
Frequently Asked Questions

What is the meaning of the song?

The song reflects themes of nostalgia and longing, capturing the essence of emotional connections.

What is the context of the song in the movie?

In Porkkaalam, the song plays during a poignant moment that highlights the protagonist's emotional struggles.

Who is the singer and music director of the song?

The song is sung by Krishnaraj and composed by Deva.

When was the song released?

The song was released on October 30, 1997.
enrichedMetadata.summary: In Thanjavooru Mannu Eduthu, the lyrics beautifully express a sense of nostalgia and longing, making it a perfect fit for moments of reflection. The song encapsulates the emotional turmoil faced by the characters in Porkkaalam, resonating with anyone who has experienced heartache. With its soothing melody and heartfelt delivery, it evokes a deep sense of yearning. This timeless piece remains a cherished classic in Tamil music history.
enrichedMetadata.high_ctr_intro: Thanjavooru Mannu Eduthu from Porkkaalam is a hauntingly beautiful melody that captures the essence of nostalgia and longing. With its evocative lyrics and soulful rendition, this song resonates deeply with anyone who has experienced the bittersweet moments of love. The collaboration between the poignant lyrics of Vairamuthu and the enchanting voice of Krishnaraj makes this track a timeless classic.
stanzaMeanings: Taking the soil from Thanjavur, releasing the water of the Thamarabarani.
Taking the soil from Thanjavur, mixing it with the water of the Thamarabarani, this is not a doll, this is the truth.
How many dolls have I made, dear? Is everything like you shining bright?
Oh, my dear, my dear, my dear cuckoo, oh my dear, my dear, my peacock.
The soil that was mixed is from Moonaru. The soil that was mixed with the eyes is from Ponnuru.
The soil that was mixed is from Maelooru. The soil that was mixed with her is from Thenoor.
The black curls mixed with the soil are from Karisa Patti. The golden hair mixed with the soil is from Sangagiri.
The beautiful soil mixed is from Vaigai Aaththu. The lovely soil mixed is from Mullaiyooru.
The soil that is mixed for the one who does the offering is from the place of the offering. Look at the soil that was mixed for the one who does the offering.
Oh, my dear, my dear, my dear cuckoo, oh my dear, my dear, my peacock.
Taking the soil from Thanjavur, releasing the water of the Thamarabarani, this is not a doll, this is the truth.
I have taken the fertile land for the harvest. I have taken the lotus field for the land.
I have taken the banana grove for the harvest. I have taken the Kanjanooru for the place.
I have taken Kanjipuram street for the hands. I have taken Srirangam for the little girl's finger.
I have taken the pattu kottai for the feet. I have taken the paanjaalam for the city.
All the villages have given shape to the body. I have given my life to give life to the eyes.
Oh, my dear, my dear, my dear cuckoo, oh my dear, my dear, my peacock.
Come on.. Taking the soil from Thanjavur, releasing the water of the Thamarabarani, this is not a doll, this is the truth.
How many dolls have I made, dear? Is everything like you shining bright?
Oh, my dear, my dear, my dear cuckoo, oh my dear, my dear, my peacock.
